items.title: Fit the Workflow, Don't Fight It
items.body: Every feature is designed around how athletic programs actually operate — not how a generic SaaS company thinks they should. We talk to ADs, coaches, and league admins constantly.

items.title: Roles Matter
items.body: A coach shouldn't see another school's full schedule. A league admin shouldn't accidentally delete a game. Access control is a core feature, not an afterthought.

items.title: Accountability Over Chaos
items.body: Every request, message, and approval creates a permanent record. "Who confirmed that game?" should have a clear answer — always.

items.title: Speed Without Shortcuts
items.body: The Group Scheduler pre-populates an entire season in minutes. But it shows you a full preview before committing a single game. Fast and careful aren't opposites.

items.title: Connected Programs
items.body: Schedules don't exist in isolation. Game Finder connects programs looking for open dates. Requests flow between schools. The network makes everyone's season better.

items.title: Less Admin, More Sport
items.body: Every hour saved on scheduling logistics is an hour someone can spend coaching, developing athletes, or running their program better. That's what we're optimizing for.

mission.heading: Give Every Athletic Program a Real Command Center
paragraphs: Athletic directors, coaches, and league admins are doing incredible work running programs that matter to their communities. They deserve software that actually fits how they operate — not generic tools bent sideways to handle a sport schedule.
ScheduleRama is purpose-built for the specific workflows, roles, and communication patterns of high school and amateur athletic programs.

cta.heading: Let's Talk About Your Program
cta.summary: We'd love to show you how ScheduleRama fits your specific setup.
cta.label: Book a Demo

story.heading: Where ScheduleRama Came From
paragraphs: It started with a frustrating email chain. A league director trying to coordinate 18 schools, 6 sports, and fall and spring seasons — tracking everything in a master spreadsheet that only one person could edit at a time. Conflicts were caught late. Games got double-booked. Coaches were working from outdated PDFs.

The tools available were either generic calendar apps that didn't understand sports structure, or enterprise systems built for professional leagues that cost more than a school's entire athletics budget.

ScheduleRama launched with a single league pilot in 2023 and has grown through direct partnerships with athletic directors and league administrators who helped shape every feature. Every workflow in the platform traces back to a real conversation with someone running a real program.
story.quote: "We needed something purpose-built. Something that understands the difference between a request and a confirmation. That knew what a league is, what a level is, what a home-and-away balance means. So we built it."

title: About

hero.heading:
Built by People Who
Run Athletic Programs
hero.summary: ScheduleRama exists because coordinating a season across dozens of schools and leagues was still being done in spreadsheets, email chains, and phone calls.

description: ScheduleRama exists because coordinating a season across dozens of schools and leagues was still being done in spreadsheets, email chains, and phone calls.

team.heading: Meet the Team — Real experience. Real solutions. Built for you.

members.name: Tito Soriano
members.bio: 20+ years working as a Software Developer in Education. With 25+ years of coaching experience, along with serving as Athletic Director, Tito has seen the headaches of managing a season firsthand. Creating an efficient, cost-saving solution to help districts is right in his wheelhouse.

members.name: Steve Mitzel
members.bio: With years of experience as a Director of Operations in education, Steve recognized the need for a solution that creates a supportive environment — one that alleviates the operational headaches that athletic departments so often face when managing a season.

members.name: Brad Eaton
members.bio: Obsessive about workflows, Brad ensures every feature starts with a conversation with an actual AD, coach, or league admin before a single line of code is written. His experience as an Athletic Director has been instrumental in shaping Schedulerama into the solution it has become.

members.name: Rosie Converse Soriano
members.bio: If you think Schedulerama looks and feels great, you can thank Rosie. As our marketing guru, she brings a sharp, creative eye and years of experience in software development, athletics, and coaching to the ScheduleRama team.
